name: amplify-set
notes: The fixed benchmark behind the B1/ISSUE-4 amplification falsifier (canon s23): does verified best-of-N beat single-pass on the local tier? Checks are DETERMINISTIC (equals/contains/contains_all over normalized output - non-model assertions, I5), so unlike golden-recall there is no human ground truth to ratify; the pre-registered thresholds below carry the decision policy, stated BEFORE any run (the C1/C2 discipline). pass@1 = mean per-task passing-candidate fraction over one N-candidate pool; pass@N = any-candidate-passes per task (what verifier-select achieves). DECISION RULE: amplify turns ON iff (pass@N - pass@1 >= b1_pass_at_n_uplift_min) AND (pass@1 <= b1_pass_at_1_headroom_max, else the set lacks headroom and the verdict is OFF-by-insufficient-evidence) AND (gen p95 <= b1_candidate_p95_ms_budget). Design: the tasks are short, objectively checkable, and instruction-strict (format compliance is part of what selection recovers).

tasks.prompt: What is 47 multiplied by 83? Reply with only the number, no thousands separators.
What is 17 percent of 3400? Reply with only the number, no punctuation.
What is 1024 minus 387? Reply with only the number, no punctuation.
What is 347 multiplied by 281? Reply with only the number, no thousands separators.
What is 7 to the power of 5? Reply with only the number, no thousands separators.
What is the remainder when 1000 is divided by 7? Reply with only the number, no punctuation.
In a non-leap year, how many days are there from March 3 to July 9 (count July 9, do not count March 3)? Reply with only the number, no punctuation.
If today is Wednesday, what day of the week is it 9 days from today? Reply with only the day name.
Spell the word 'keel' backwards. Reply with only the reversed word, lowercase, no punctuation.
How many times does the letter r appear in the text 'strawberry raspberry'? Reply with only the number, no punctuation.
Sort these words alphabetically: mango kiwi apple fig. Reply with the four words in order, separated by single spaces.
What is the third word of this sentence: 'the quick brown fox jumps'? Reply with only that word, no punctuation.
How many vowels (a, e, i, o, u only) are in the word 'sovereignty'? Reply with only the number, no punctuation.
Spell the word 'harness' backwards. Reply with only the reversed word, lowercase.
Anna is taller than Ben. Ben is taller than Cy. Who is the shortest? Reply with only the name, no punctuation.
A bat and a ball cost 1.10 in total. The bat costs exactly 1.00 more than the ball. How many cents does the ball cost? Reply with only the number, no punctuation.
How many legs do 3 spiders and 2 birds have in total? Reply with only the number, no punctuation.
Mary's father has exactly five daughters: Nana, Nene, Nini, Nono, and one more. What is the fifth daughter's name? Reply with only the name, no punctuation.
Reply with exactly this string and nothing else: KEEL-OK-042
Output the word anchor exactly three times, joined by single hyphens, nothing else.
Reply with a single JSON object that has exactly one key named port whose integer value is 8080. No prose, no code fences.
Reply with the single word: unsinkable
From this text: 'Order #7741 shipped to Oslo on May 2'. What is the order number? Reply with only the number, no punctuation.
From this text: 'server listening on 127.0.0.1:9443'. What is the port? Reply with only the number, no punctuation.
From this text: 'the invoice totals 4,250 SEK'. What is the currency code? Reply with only the three-letter code, no punctuation.
